chainSession.title: Set mining session SOL
chainSession.body: Mining can create or expand Solana chunk storage accounts. This SOL is sent from your wallet to your browser session signer so mining can submit transactions without asking you to sign every block.
chainSession.pointStorage: The spending is Solana network cost: transaction fees and rent-exempt storage deposits for chunk PDAs.
chainSession.pointProject: It is not paid to the NiceChunk project wallet, and the project cannot withdraw your session balance.
chainSession.pointControl: Your main wallet only signs this setup or a later top-up; block mining is then signed by your temporary session key.
chainSession.mobileBody: Mobile browser signing requires opening this page inside a wallet app browser.

siteFooter.tagline: A seeded voxel world protocol on Solana.

hero.title: Guardian Network Console
hero.body: Register a non-custodial Guardian to keep a region reachable, synchronize realtime chunk activity, and support local game services. Stable Guardians can earn NCK from future regional resource taxes and trading taxes while never holding player assets or signing for users.

register.title: Register Node
register.body: Publish a connection endpoint for a 100 x 100 chunk service region. Offline or unstable nodes can be penalized; stable nodes can receive Guardian rewards from the treasury over time, paid back to the registration wallet.

status.noWallet: No Solana wallet was detected. Install Phantom or Solflare.
status.confirmWallet: Confirm the registration transaction in your wallet.
status.updateConfirm: Confirm the endpoint update in your wallet.

list.title: Active Guardians
list.body: Live devnet Guardian regions. Connect a wallet to prioritize and mark your own Guardians.
list.registryMissing: Guardian registry has not been initialized yet.
list.connectFirst: Connect a wallet to load the Guardian registry.
list.empty: No active guardians are registered yet.

access.title: Access Layer
access.body: Guardians keep regional endpoints reachable so clients can discover and communicate with nearby chunk services.

realtime.title: Realtime Transport
realtime.body: They support live coordination for region activity without replacing wallet signatures or on-chain settlement.

nonCustodial.title: Non-Custodial Flow
nonCustodial.body: Guardians never custody player assets, sign for users, or process Solana transactions on a player's behalf.

error.insufficientSol: The connected wallet needs a small amount of devnet SOL for transaction fees.
error.insufficientNck: The connected wallet does not have enough devnet NCK for the 100,000 NCK registration payment.
error.noPriorCredit: The wallet has no devnet SOL record yet. Request devnet SOL before registering.
error.invalidTokenAccount: The NCK token account is invalid. Confirm that the wallet holds devnet NCK.
error.regionAlreadyActive: This region already has an active Guardian.
error.invalidConnection: Invalid connection endpoint. Use ws://host:port or wss://host:port without a path.
error.invalidHost: Invalid host. Use only a domain, IP address, or hostname without http:// or wss://.
error.missingAdjacentGuardian: A non-genesis Guardian must be adjacent to at least one active Guardian.
error.invalidAdjacentGuardian: Adjacent region PDA is invalid. Refresh and try again.
error.noGenesisPermission: Genesis Guardian registration must be signed by the development wallet. Switch wallets or disable genesis registration.
error.genesisAlreadyRegistered: The Genesis Guardian has already been registered.

page.title: NCM On-chain 3D Asset Format
page.description: NCM is NiceChunk's compact Solana game asset format. Store voxel 3D models as PDA-friendly chain text without IPFS.

hero.title: We designed NCM so Solana games can own their 3D assets on-chain.
hero.body: NCM is NiceChunk's compact 3D asset format for Solana and other chains. We are building deeper R&D around this format because it can compress voxel assets into tiny text records that clients fetch directly through RPC, verify publicly, and keep available without IPFS.
hero.formatBody: Palette, box count, signed positions, and dimensions encoded as compact varints.

rpc.title: RPC native
rpc.body: A client can read the text from account data and reconstruct the model immediately.

public.title: Public forever
public.body: The asset is stored with the program state, not hidden behind a mutable external gateway.

compact.title: Cost aware
compact.body: Same-color voxels are merged into boxes before encoding to reduce storage size.

portable.title: Chain portable
portable.body: NCM is plain text, so the same asset format can be used beyond one runtime.

thesis.title: A Solana game asset should be part of the state, not a URL attached to it.
thesis.body: NiceChunk is building around NCM because a voxel object can be small enough to live directly inside a program-owned PDA. The asset, its rules, and its ownership can move together, so a game item or NFT does not depend on an external gateway staying online.

pda.title: PDA-native resources
pda.body: NCM turns small characters, props, equipment, and block decorations into account data that Solana clients can read through normal RPC calls.

trade.title: Safer NFT trading
trade.body: When the visual asset is stored with the asset record, buyers do not need to trust that an IPFS gateway, metadata server, or mutable JSON link will keep resolving later.

research.title: Useful compression research
research.body: NCM is also the base for our NCM-DNA exploration: searching for shorter deterministic genes that reproduce the same model and can turn asset compression into useful computation.

converter.title: Browser local converter
converter.body: Upload a MagicaVoxel .vox file. The file is parsed locally in your browser and converted into NCM text.
converter.dropBody: MagicaVoxel SIZE, XYZI, and RGBA chunks are supported.

license.title: Sample attribution
license.body: Sample VOX characters are from OpenGameArt, created by JoshMckay, licensed under CC-BY 4.0.

output.title: Compact chain text
output.body: Store this payload in account data or a program-owned asset record. Clients can decode it without IPFS.

page.title: NCM DNA Lab
page.description: NCM DNA Lab searches for deterministic short genes that can grow into the same canonical cuboid model.

hero.title: NCM DNA Lab
hero.body: NCM DNA Lab is an experimental gene search system for on-chain 3D assets. Instead of storing every cube, it searches for a shorter deterministic gene that can grow into the same model.
hero.pow: In the future, this search can become a useful Proof of Work: miners spend CPU power to discover shorter genes, reduce PDA storage size, and earn rewards from the storage cost saved.

cpu.title: Why CPU-first?
cpu.body: NCM-DNA v1 uses a rule-based cuboid growth language instead of GPU-friendly voxel functions. The search contains branching, templates, symmetry detection, canonical sorting, and variable-length genes. These operations are more suitable for general CPU execution and harder to fully parallelize on GPU.

chain.title: Storage-saving submission path
chain.step1: Asset PDA stores canonical_model_hash and current_encoding.
chain.step3: Program verifies grow(gene) hash equals canonical_model_hash.
chain.step4: If gene is shorter, asset encoding is updated.
chain.step5: Released PDA rent can be shared between miner, owner, and protocol pool.

log.title: Worker events
log.ncmTargetLoaded: NCM character target loaded with {boxes} cuboids.
log.continuousStarted: Continuous search started across {threads} worker threads.

page.title: NiceChunk Resource Rules
page.heading: Public rules for block-to-resource discovery.
page.body: NiceChunk resource rules describe how destroyed blocks become materials. This page exposes the current rule manifest, a deterministic simulator, and the event shape that can later map to verifiable chain logic.
page.conditionValue: Only destroyed stone blocks produce this resource.
page.resultNoReward: {material} is not eligible for this rule.
page.flowTitle: How the test maps to the future contract
page.flow1: The client submits the destroyed block coordinate and material.
page.flow2: The simulator checks whether the material is stone.
page.flow3: If eligible, it returns a deterministic resource reward.
page.flow4: The game hotbar clears non-tool items and records the produced resource.

page.title: NiceChunk Box Resource Atlas
page.heading: Every generated block is a public resource rule.
page.body: NiceChunk maps terrain blocks into scientifically scoped resource foundations. Each block exposes its generation signal, likely elemental composition range, and role in the future mining and forging economy.
page.modelBody: Ranges are material tendencies, not fixed rewards. Actual resource rolls can later use seed, coordinate, depth, biome, and chain-verified state.
page.empty: No generated blocks match the current filters.

grass.name: Grass
grass.description: A living topsoil block: silicate soil under a thin biological layer shaped by humidity, slope, and forest density.

dirt.name: Dirt
dirt.description: Weathered soil below grass, mixing silicate grains, clay minerals, iron oxides, moisture, and organic residue.

stone.name: Stone
stone.description: Common silicate rock used as the baseline geological material for hills, cliffs, cave walls, and structural mining.

deepStone.name: Deep Stone
deepStone.description: Dense lower-strata rock with higher iron and magnesium tendency, used by the generator below the shallow crust.

sand.name: Sand
sand.description: Quartz-dominant granular sediment, produced by desert, beach, river, lake, and ocean-floor rules.

gravel.name: Gravel
gravel.description: Mixed fragmented rock grains; composition varies strongly by nearby terrain but stays silicate-dominant.

clay.name: Clay
clay.description: Fine hydrated aluminosilicate sediment generated in wet, low-slope terrain and water-adjacent strata.

mud.name: Mud
mud.description: Water-heavy soil with organic content, found in low wet biomes where drainage and oxygen exposure are limited.

dryDirt.name: Dry Dirt
dryDirt.description: Desiccated soil with reduced hydrogen and organic fractions, produced by low-humidity surface rules.

saltFlat.name: Salt Flat
saltFlat.description: Evaporite crust left by dry basins; sodium chloride dominates with magnesium, calcium, sulfate, and trapped oxygen-bearing salts.

snow.name: Snow
snow.description: Frozen precipitation layer generated above snowline or in cold biome conditions, with trace mineral dust possible.

ice.name: Ice
ice.description: Solid water phase selected by cold fluid and rare snowfield rules; chemically simple but environmentally important.

frozenSoil.name: Frozen Soil
frozenSoil.description: Permafrost-like soil that mixes mineral grains, bound water ice, and preserved organic matter.

basalt.name: Basalt
basalt.description: Dark mafic volcanic rock formed from fast-cooled lava, with stronger iron, magnesium, calcium, and titanium tendency than common stone.

ash.name: Ash
ash.description: Fine volcanic particulate generated in volcanic biomes, chemically similar to silicate rock but more porous and sulfate-prone.

bedrock.name: Bedrock
bedrock.description: Protocol foundation layer at the base of generated columns. It behaves like hard basement rock, not a fixed chemical resource deposit.

water.name: Water
water.description: Standard fluid block for oceans, rivers, lakes, and lowland water coverage, with minor dissolved ion variance by biome.

swampWater.name: Swamp Water
swampWater.description: Organic-rich stagnant water generated by swamp and wetland rules; chemistry includes dissolved carbon, sulfur, iron, and nitrogen traces.

toxicWater.name: Toxic Water
toxicWater.description: Hazard fluid selected by corruption signals. Its base remains water, but trace metals and sulfides raise biological risk.

lava.name: Lava
lava.description: Molten silicate fluid generated by volcanic signals. Composition follows basaltic melt rather than generic fantasy fire.

quicksand.name: Quicksand
quicksand.description: Water-loosened sand trap generated by desert weirdness; mostly silica with enough water to reduce mechanical stability.

trunk.name: Trunk
trunk.description: Woody structural tissue generated by broadleaf tree rules, dominated by cellulose, lignin, water, and mineral nutrients.

leaves.name: Leaves
leaves.description: Photosynthetic canopy blocks, chemically organic with magnesium-bearing chlorophyll and nutrient trace metals.

pineTrunk.name: Pine Trunk
pineTrunk.description: Resin-rich conifer wood generated by cold, high, or evergreen tree rules.

pineLeaves.name: Pine Leaves
pineLeaves.description: Evergreen needle canopy with lower water turnover and mineral nutrient traces.

deadWood.name: Dead Wood
deadWood.description: Desiccated or heat-stressed wood with reduced hydrogen and higher exposed carbon fraction.

giantRoot.name: Giant Root
giantRoot.description: Large exposed root mass generated in humid rainforest rules; structurally woody but wetter and mineral-rich near soil contact.

grassPlant.name: Grass Plant
grassPlant.description: Small herbaceous plant selected by humid low-slope surface rules.

dryGrass.name: Dry Grass
dryGrass.description: Dried herbaceous plant material from low-humidity surfaces, with lower hydrogen and higher carbon concentration.

bush.name: Bush
bush.description: Woody shrub layer generated by forest, plains, mountain, and rainforest vegetation rules.

deadBush.name: Dead Bush
deadBush.description: Dried shrub remnant generated by desert and volcanic surface conditions.

cactus.name: Cactus
cactus.description: Water-storing succulent generated by desert rules, chemically organic but with unusually high bound water fraction.

reed.name: Reed
reed.description: Water-edge plant generated near swamp, river, lake, and wetland fluids.

swampGrass.name: Swamp Grass
swampGrass.description: Wetland grass layer with organic tissue and sediment contamination from stagnant water.

snowBush.name: Snow Bush
snowBush.description: Cold-resistant shrub, often tinted by snow or frost and selected by mountain and snowfield vegetation rules.

thorn.name: Thorn
thorn.description: Hardened defensive plant material from dry or volcanic surfaces, with concentrated carbon-rich structural tissue.

moss.name: Moss
moss.description: Moist non-woody biological mat generated by humid forest, swamp, and cave-adjacent rules.

lichen.name: Lichen
lichen.description: Symbiotic crust on cold or rocky surfaces, bridging biological tissue and mineral substrate.

vine.name: Vine
vine.description: Climbing plant generated in humid biomes and cave-like damp areas, with flexible carbon-rich tissue.

glowMycelium.name: Glow Mycelium
glowMycelium.description: Bioluminescent fungal mat from subterranean humid spaces. Its chemistry is biological with phosphorus and sulfur signal emphasis.

mushroom.name: Mushroom
mushroom.description: Fungal fruiting body selected by rare humid vegetation rolls.

seaweed.name: Seaweed
seaweed.description: Marine plant-like biomass from underwater regions, with sodium, potassium, magnesium, and iodine trace tendencies.

aquaticPlant.name: Aquatic Plant
aquaticPlant.description: Freshwater vegetation with high water content and nutrient uptake from sediment.

coral.name: Coral
coral.description: Calcium carbonate structure generated in warm shallow ocean conditions.

deadCoral.name: Dead Coral
deadCoral.description: Dead calcium carbonate reef material selected by cold or corrupted ocean rules.

shellBed.name: Shell Bed
shellBed.description: Accumulated shell fragments and carbonate grains generated as rare coastal and ocean-floor speckles.

H.short: Hydrogen is a nonmetal resource foundation used for fuel cells, hydrogen storage, life chemistry, rocket propellant, and organic synthesis in NiceChunk's material and forging vocabulary.
H.gameplayRole: In NiceChunk, Hydrogen should be balanced as a nonmetal material: it defines processing routes, affinity rules, visual resource genes, and player-facing risk or value when discovered from seeded chunks.

page.title: NiceChunk Elements
page.heading: Elements become resources.
page.body: NiceChunk uses the 118 real chemical elements as the open vocabulary for future resources, forging, material visuals, and verifiable game rules.

resourceGene.title: Resource Gene
resourceGene.body: Each element can become a deterministic material identity for mined drops, block recipes, and future asset metadata.

forgingInput.title: Forging Input
forgingInput.body: Atomic and physical traits map into future processing tiers, heat requirements, risks, and station logic.

visualGene.title: Visual Gene
visualGene.body: Color, metallic, roughness, emission, vein, grain, and crack traits can drive voxel material rendering.

verification.title: Open Verification
verification.body: The table is designed as public rule data so players can inspect how resources are classified before chain commitments.

page.detailsTitle: Click an element to inspect its properties and processing requirements.
page.detailsBody: The requirements below are game-facing placeholders. They define how each element category can later become mined resources, processed materials, and new constructible blocks.
page.summary: {name} is element {atomicNumber}. In NiceChunk it represents a {category} resource that can be discovered from block destruction and later used in block construction rules.
page.constructionHint: {name} can be used as a base {category} component when future recipes define new constructible blocks.

detail.missingZh: Element detail data has not been added yet.
detail.missingEn: Element detail data has not been added yet.

forging.title: Nicechunk Forging Bench
forging.chainCodeHint: This compact code stores the forged components, offsets, dimensions, materials, and voxel cuts.
forging.chainCodePlaceholder: Paste a chain code to generate an editable workpiece.

status.castLocked: Cast item moves as one piece

status.label: Status
status.noTarget: Aim at a glowing ore vein

hero.title: Proof of Short Seed
hero.subtitle: Miners compete to find the shortest valid seed for the current world contour.
hero.body: NiceChunk miners are not searching for meaningless nonces. They search for a Shape Seed that reconstructs the current world contour. More complex contours are harder to describe with a short seed. The first miner to submit a seed that is short enough under the current difficulty solves the epoch and earns the simulated reward.

boundary.title: Frontier Target
boundary.body: The current world contour is canonicalized into target_frontier_root.

context.title: Short Seed Mining
context.body: Miners compete to find a short Shape Seed that reconstructs the current contour.

meaningful.title: First Valid Wins
meaningful.body: The first valid seed accepted for the epoch wins the simulated block reward.

tiny.title: Auto Retarget
tiny.body: Every minute the max_seed_bytes difficulty adjusts to keep blocks near 60 seconds.

grid.title: Current Frontier Target
grid.canvas: Proof of Short Seed world grid

context.title: Neighbor Context
context.note: Generated from unlocked N/E/S/W Chunk Keys in a fixed order.

worldState.title: World State
worldState.seedTargetValue: Harder left: max {max} bytes / RAW {raw} bytes

contour.title: Contour Encoding
contour.body: DecodeShapeSeed v1 supports RAW_EDGES, PATH_RLE, and RECT. RAW_EDGES is the fallback: once max_seed_bytes reaches the raw length, any finite contour is guaranteed solvable.
contour.pastePlaceholder: Paste NSS1|RAW|..., NSS1|PATH|..., or NSS1|RECT|... here.

formulas.title: seed_bytes <= max_seed_bytes
formulas.body: The demo does not prove a seed is globally shortest. It proves the seed reconstructs the current Frontier Root, is canonical, and is short enough under the current max_seed_bytes difficulty.

advanced.title: Commit-reveal for production
advanced.body: Direct reveal is simple for the demo. Production should commit first, then reveal seed and salt after a short delay to prevent another user from copying a valid seed and submitting it first.

transactions.title: SeedBlock Events
transactions.empty: No seed blocks yet. Search and submit a valid seed to solve the first epoch.

status.autoRunning: Auto running: searching and submitting valid seeds.
status.noValidSeed: No valid seed under current max_seed_bytes.
status.rootMismatch: Seed does not reconstruct the current frontier.
status.seedTooLong: Seed is valid but too long for current difficulty.
status.relaxed: No block for 10 minutes. Seed length target relaxed.

messages.locked: This chunk has no unlocked neighbors. Frontier proof requires at least one confirmed neighbor key.
messages.unlocked: This chunk is already unlocked. Its key can now be used by adjacent chunks.
messages.candidate: This chunk can be mined because it touches the current world frontier.
messages.decoded: Decoded {count} frontier edges from the Contour Seed and highlighted the boundary.
messages.invalidSeed: This Contour Seed could not be decoded. Paste a valid nckc_ seed.
messages.copyFailed: Copy failed in this browser context.
